title: cymbachne guineensis subvar. infrasulcata
intro: Cymbachne guineensis subvar. infrasulcata, also known as the Guinea cymbachne or African grass, is a type of grass that is native to tropical Africa and is characterized by its distinctive, rounded leaves and robust growth habit. Traditionally, the Guinea cymbachne has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The health benefits of Guinea cymbachne include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and fighting off bacterial and fungal infections. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of the Guinea cymbachne can be used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions that are often used to treat various ailments.
